title: Old Dominion Freight Line to Webcast Second Quarter 2022 Conference Call
text: THOMASVILLE, N.C.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Old Dominion Freight Line, Inc. (Nasdaq: ODFL) announced today that it plans to release its second quarter 2022 financial results before opening of trading on Wednesday, July 27, 2022. The Company will also hold a conference call to discuss its financial results and outlook at 10:00 a.m. (Eastern Time) on Wednesday, July 27, 2022. An online, real-time webcast of Old Dominion's quarterly conference call will be available at www.odfl.com on Wednesday, July 27,

title: Old Dominion's Potential As A Dividend Growth Stock Is Phenomenal
text: I start this article by sharing my view on the macroeconomic situation as we're dealing with a post-pandemic bullwhip effect and lower demand. Then, I explain why Old Dominion is a top-tier dividend growth stock thanks to its business model and ability to generate shareholder value.

title: Solid LTL Segment Aids Old Dominion (ODFL), High Costs Ail
text: Strong performance of the LTL segment and an improved operating ratio boosts Old Dominion (ODFL). However, increased operating expenses are a concern.

title: These Dividend Stocks Can Double Your Money in Under 5 Years
text: These exceptional stocks represent the convergence of rising revenues, growing dividends, and unique operating niches.

title: Old Dominion (ODFL) Posts Solid May LTL Unit Performance
text: Old Dominion's (ODFL) revenue per day climbs 26% year over year in May 2022 on a 2.3% increase in LTL tons per day and a rise in LTL revenue per hundredweight.

title: Old Dominion update points to strong Q2
text: Old Dominion Freight Line announced Monday that revenue increased 26% year-over-year on a per-day basis in May. The carrier previously disclosed a 29.1% increase for the month of April.

title: Old Dominion Freight Line Provides Update for Second Quarter 2022
text: THOMASVILLE, N.C.--(BUSINESS WIRE)---- $ODFL--Old Dominion Freight Line, Inc. (Nasdaq: ODFL) today reported certain less-than-truckload (“LTL”) operating metrics for May 2022. Revenue per day increased 26.0% as compared to May 2021 due to a 2.3% increase in LTL tons per day and an increase in LTL revenue per hundredweight. The change in LTL tons per day was attributable to a 2.8% increase in LTL shipments per day that was partially offset by a 0.6% decrease in LTL weight per shipment. For the quarter-to-
